habitat: Open woodland; forest edges; parks; gardens; mangroves; and riverine habitats
story: Meet the Oriental Dollarbird (Eurystomus orientalis), a distinctive bird from the Coraciidae family. Preferring habitats such as Open woodland; forest edges; parks; gardens; mangroves; and riverine habitats, this species has adapted to thrive under specific environmental conditions. With an IUCN status of Least Concern (LC), its population trends are closely watched by scientists and enthusiasts. The Oriental Dollarbird serves as an indicator species for the health of its environment. Whether you are a seasoned ornithologist or a curious nature lover, learning about the Oriental Dollarbird enriches our understanding of nature.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
